name: Em
description_short: EM Federal Credit Union is a member-owned credit union in Mesa, AZ offering auto loans, signature loans, and recreational vehicle financing with competitive rates.
description_long: EM Federal Credit Union is a federally-chartered credit union headquartered in Mesa, Arizona at 50 West Iron Ave. The institution operates as a member-owned, not-for-profit financial cooperative insured by the NCUA, with savings federally insured to at least $250,000. EM Federal Credit Union specializes in consumer lending across multiple vehicle categories. They offer unsecured signature loans up to $5,500 for personal expenses like home improvements or vacations. Their primary focus is auto financing, with advertised rates as low as 4.49% APR for up to 72 months and 4.74% APR for up to 84 months. The credit union also finances motorcycles, ATVs, dirt bikes, travel trailers, RVs, and toy haulers. Additionally, Empire employees are eligible for a line of credit up to $4,500 specifically for tool purchases through Empire Southwest Parts Department. EM Federal Credit Union distinguishes itself through competitive vehicle financing rates and membership benefits tied to specific employers, particularly Empire Southwest. The credit union maintains limited branch hours (Monday-Friday 11am-4pm) and provides member assistance through a dedicated phone line. Their focus on recreational vehicle financing and specialized lending products for member groups reflects a niche market approach rather than full-service banking. As a credit union, EM Federal offers the advantages of member ownership and not-for-profit operation, typically resulting in lower loan rates and fewer fees than traditional banks. However, the website provides minimal information about savings products, membership requirements, or other services beyond lending. The limited hours of operation and single physical location may present accessibility challenges for some potential members. The company's marketing emphasizes vehicle financing heavily, suggesting this is their core business focus.
services: New and used car financing with rates from 4.49% APR
Unsecured signature loans up to $5,500 for personal purchases or home improvements
Employer-based tool purchase line of credit (up to $4,500 for Empire employees)
Member assistance and information services via phone
Member login and online account access
pros: Competitive auto loan rates starting as low as 4.49% APR for up to 72 months
Member-owned credit union structure typically provides lower fees and rates than traditional banks
NCUA-insured savings up to at least $250,000
Unsecured signature loans available up to $5,500 with no collateral required
Specialized financing for recreational vehicles (RVs, travel trailers, motorcycles, ATVs)
Employer-specific lending programs (up to $4,500 tool financing for Empire employees)
Federal credit union status with full faith and credit of U.S. Government backing
cons: Limited hours of operation (Monday-Friday 11am-4pm only) restrict member access
Single physical location in Mesa, Arizona limits convenience for non-local members
Website provides minimal information about membership eligibility requirements or application process
No mention of online banking, mobile app, or digital services capabilities
Limited product information - savings accounts, CDs, and other services barely mentioned on website
best_for: Vehicle buyers (new/used cars, motorcycles, RVs) seeking competitive auto loan rates
Empire Southwest employees needing specialized tool purchase financing
Arizona residents seeking membership in a member-owned credit union
Borrowers with good credit seeking unsecured personal loans up to $5,500
diagnosis: EM Federal Credit Union is best for vehicle buyers in the Mesa, Arizona area seeking competitive auto loan rates and members of eligible employer groups. The main caveat is that the credit union operates with very limited hours (11am-4pm weekdays only) and maintains a single location, making it primarily suitable for local members or those able to conduct most banking by phone.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.

meta_description: Em — NCUA-insured credit union in MESA, AZ. Member-owned, not-for-profit. Loans, savings, and member services.
